D4 Colorimetry L5 Evaluate Reference

PQ vs HLG

overlay Rec.2100's two HDR transfer curves and see why one is absolute and display-referred while the other stays relative and SDR-compatible

Rec.2100 offers two HDR transfer functions. PQ (ST 2084) is absolute: a code value maps to an exact luminance in cd/m², spending most codes on the dark end. HLG is relative: a square-root toe identical to legacy gamma keeps it backward-compatible with SDR screens, with a log shoulder for highlights.

Both curves map input light (as a fraction of peak) to a 0–1 signal. Slide the light level and read each code. Watch PQ rise far steeper in the shadows; HLG's lower toe matches an ordinary gamma curve, so SDR displays show a watchable picture.
